title: Impatiens lizipingensis
intro: Impatiens lizipingensis is a species of plant in the family Balsamaceae, commonly known as Liziping balsam or Liziping touch-me-not. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Impatiens lizipingensis include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, often used to treat respiratory infections and fever.
taxonomy: Impatiens lizipingensis is a species of plant in the family Balsamaceae. It is a type of balsam, which is a flowering plant commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant's classification places it within the genus Impatiens, which consists of over 1,000 species of plants. Impatiens lizipingensis is typically characterized by its bright colors and delicate appearance. As a member of the Balsamaceae family, it shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as the balsam poplar.
